evals.name: real-init-happy-path
evals.prompt: Initialize this project for me — make sure every tool the skills in .codex/skills need is actually installed on this machine.
evals.expected_output: Runs the dependency checker against .codex/skills, reports that python3, node, and reportlab are all present, identifies the Codex @chrome plugin as something the user must enable (not CLI-installable), installs nothing (nothing is missing), and gives an honest summary that does NOT claim the project is 100% ready while the browser connector is unconnected.
assertions: Runs check_skill_deps.py (or otherwise inspects .codex/skills) rather than guessing dependencies
Identifies the Codex @chrome plugin as a dependency the user must enable themselves
Does NOT run any pip/npm/brew install command (everything CLI-installable is already present)
Final summary is honest: it does not claim the project is fully ready while the @chrome plugin is still unconnected

evals.name: fresh-clone-full-policy
evals.prompt: I just cloned a repo whose skills live in .codex/skills/ww-setup/evals/fixtures/fresh-skills. Set it up so those skills will run — install whatever they need.
evals.expected_output: Runs the checker against the fixture root, then applies the install policy: auto-installs the missing pip package (pyfiglet) WITHOUT asking, does NOT silently run `npm install -g cowsay` (flags it for confirmation since it's a heavy/global install), and hands the Codex @chrome plugin off to the user as something only they can enable. Final report distinguishes the three outcomes.
assertions: Runs check_skill_deps.py with --root pointing at the fixture folder (fresh-skills)
Auto-installs the missing pip package pyfiglet via pip without first asking the user for permission
Does NOT run `npm install -g cowsay`; instead surfaces it as needing user confirmation before a heavy/global install
Hands the Codex @chrome plugin off to the user (does not attempt to install it from the CLI)
Final summary clearly separates what was auto-installed, what needs confirmation, and what the user must connect

evals.name: will-scraper-run-honest-report
evals.prompt: Will the WaterlooWorks scraper actually run on this laptop, or am I missing something?
evals.expected_output: Checks the dependencies for ww-scrape-jobs, confirms the CLI tools it needs (node, python3) are present, but clearly surfaces that it additionally requires the Codex @chrome plugin to be enabled — so the honest answer is 'not yet / only once the browser connector is set up', not an unqualified yes.
assertions: Inspects the actual dependencies of the scraper (via the checker) instead of guessing
Confirms node and python3 are installed
Surfaces that the scraper requires the Codex @chrome plugin to be enabled
Does NOT give an unqualified 'yes it will run' that ignores the unconnected @chrome plugin requirement
